title: Tinder, WeWard, Lime: French government tests new tools to fight voter abstention
subhead: To encourage French citizens, especially young people, to vote in the municipal elections on March 15 and 22, the government is multiplying partnerships with private platforms. The latest: WeWard, an app that encourages walking to polling stations.

title: Middle East war: Gulf economies on the brink of suffocation
subhead: Although the six Gulf countries have diversified their economies, hydrocarbons still account for about 30% of their gross domestic product (GDP). The blockade of the Strait of Hormuz is also preventing them from importing goods, while the region relies on imports for 85% of its food supply.

title: France beats England 48-46 to claim back-to-back Six Nations titles
subhead: Louis Bielle-Biarrey scored four of the 13 tries in a barn-storming encounter in which the lead changed hands numerous times. Meanwhile, Wales tasted Six Nations success for the first time in 1,099 days after blowing away Italy 31-17 at Principality Stadium on Saturday.

title: 'We are fighting absolute evil': General Eyal Zamir's 'existential war' as Israeli army's chief of general staff
subhead: The military officer, 60, who took office on March 5 and does not hesitate to voice his disagreement with certain decisions made by Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's government, enjoyed widespread popularity in a country where the army is the backbone of society.

title: Tradition done right: Five Paris restaurants reimagining the classics
subhead: Are you a fan of French cuisine classics such as 'œufs en meurette' (poached eggs in red wine sauce), 'navarin d'agneau' (lamb stew) and apple tart? Discover our selection of five Paris restaurants that awaken both the appetite and a sense of nostalgia.

subhead: Philosopher Stéphanie Roza and political scientist Amirpasha Tavakkoli explain how, throughout the 20th century, the political turmoil in Iran, torn between modernity and anti-modernity, ultimately led to Islamist victory.

title: Pete Hegseth, the embodiment of Trump's war in Iran
subhead: The US secretary of defense, a former Fox News host, has ardently defended the operation against the Islamic Republic. He has disregarded rules of engagement and boasted about American supremacy.

title: Benjamin Netanyahu, a prime minister without checks and balances
subhead: The strikes in Iran have further reduced the political space for opposition in Israel, even as concerns mount over a possible ground offensive in Lebanon. Today, only Donald Trump seems capable of influencing the Israeli leader.
